page: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an arm64-based virtual server powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 8 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz with 1.0 thread per core, paired with 16.0 GB of memory, which equates to approximately 1.95 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 160 GB SSD. The instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and operates under a QEMU hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across several standard compute workloads. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests fall into the average category, with scores of 1740.19 and 14455.92 respectively. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 560.18 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 79.75 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, the server exhibits weak performance in single-core Geekbench tests (1063.0) and Passmark memory scores (1643.91). Large-block memory writes (45.15 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression are also weak.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff for sustained high-compute tasks, as evidenced by poor LLM text generation speeds of 31.49 tokens per second on a small 135M model. Despite these limitations, the instance achieves average results in database operations with a Passmark database score of 5418.51 and a static web serving throughput of 8.6 GB/sec. This hardware profile is suitable for lightweight web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations where dedicated CPU resources are not strictly required.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an arm64 virtual server featuring 8 shared vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 160 GB of local SSD storage, with no integrated GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks show average capabilities for general computing, including a multi-core stress-ng score of 14455.92 and a static web serving throughput of 8.6 GB/sec. However, the shared CPU architecture and weak memory performance limit its suitability for intensive workloads like LLM inference, where text generation speeds are poor. This instance is best suited for cost-efficient web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an arm64 server featuring 8 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage, offering average performance for web serving and database operations.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cax31 is an arm64 server with 8 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, designed for basic web serving.
tagline: An arm64 virtual server with shared Ampere Altra processors and local SSD storage for budget-conscious general workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local SSD storage
Average performance in static web serving and database operations
Weak single-core CPU and memory benchmark results
Suited for software compilation, lightweight web servers, and development environments
